recipe.title: Vanilla Layer Cake with Rose Buttercream

ingredients.name: rose powder
ingredients.note: optional in batter; 1/2 tsp+ in frosting

steps.visualCue: 325F oven, four 6x2-inch pans greased and parchment-lined
steps.textureCue: matte parchment circles on metal pans
steps.runwayRisk: wrong pan count or diameter drift

steps.visualCue: flour, sugar, leavening sandy mix in stand mixer bowl
steps.textureCue: fine even powder, no butter yet

steps.visualCue: butter cubes disappear into sandy crumbs
steps.textureCue: coarse meal with pea-sized butter flecks
steps.runwayRisk: melted butter smear instead of crumbs

steps.text: Add eggs one at a time
steps.visualCue: pale yellow batter thickens between eggs

steps.text: Whisk wet mix with rose and vanilla
steps.visualCue: pink-tinged milk swirl with rose powder and vanilla specks
steps.textureCue: blush milk vein, floral dust motes
steps.runwayRisk: hot magenta instead of pale blush

steps.text: Add wet ingredients in three parts
steps.visualCue: batter turns glossy pale ivory, scrape-down between additions
pans two-thirds full, golden domed layers, clean toothpick
steps.textureCue: springy golden top, fine velvety crumb edge
steps.runwayRisk: single thick loaf instead of four disks

steps.visualCue: wire leveler shaves dome; flat stackable disks
steps.textureCue: satisfying curl of golden crumb falling away

steps.visualCue: pale butter to airy blush peaks after sugar alternation
steps.textureCue: satin glossy peaks, pipeable not greasy
steps.runwayRisk: split greasy frosting or neon pink

steps.text: Stack, crumb coat, final coat, decorate
steps.visualCue: four layers on stand, thin filling squeeze, smooth final shell, rosette border
steps.textureCue: crumb coat texture vs mirror-smooth final pink shell
steps.runwayRisk: layer count drift, leaning tower, slice before hero lock

assumptions: Video uses four 6-inch (15 cm) diameter by 2-inch (5 cm) cake layers for a tall vertical stack readable in 9:16, matching the source's four-pan option rather than two 8-inch layers.

Finished hero is a four-layer round cake on a white cake stand on Licorn terrazzo, about 15 cm diameter and 12-14 cm tall with frosting, pale blush buttercream, simple piped rosette border (source says decorate as desired).
Rose buttercream stays pale blush pink, not hot magenta; rose powder is subtle floral, not overpowering.
Food-porn ASMR pacing: texture-first opening on rose-tinted milk swirl; no ingredient lineup; final slice cross-section only in segment-06, never as hook.
Oven shots use already-open Licorn oven references; no door choreography.
criticalTransformations: Rose powder dissolving into milk with blush vein and vanilla speckle
Dry sandy mix to butter-cut crumbs to glossy ribbon batter
Pale batter in pan to golden domed baked layer with fine velvety crumb
Domed layer shaved to flat stackable disk with crumb curl payoff
Pale butter whipping to airy blush satin peaks for rose buttercream
Thin frosting layer squeeze between stacked disks
Crumb coat speckled shell hard-cutting to mirror-smooth final pink coat
Optional wedge slice revealing four even sponge layers and blush filling
visualTextureOpportunities: Rose powder dust hitting milk with swirling blush vein
Vanilla bean paste dark flecks streaking through pink milk
Butter cubes dropping into flour cloud in mixer bowl
Glossy batter ribbon falling from paddle scrape
Toothpick emerging clean from golden cake center
Steaming layer releasing from pan onto cooling rack
Wire leveler shaving dome with golden crumb curl falling
Buttercream whisk lifting pale pink glossy peaks
Offset spatula first frosting smear and filling squeeze between layers
Piping bag rosette kiss on cake border
Cross-section of four sponge layers with blush buttercream bands
possibleHooks: Rose powder whisked into milk: blush spiral paid off by vanilla streak (texture-first opening)
Glossy batter ribbon cascade from stand mixer paddle
Wire leveler shaving golden dome with satisfying crumb curl
Pale butter whipping to airy blush buttercream peaks
First offset spatula swipe of pink frosting on naked cake layer

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the hero cake use four 6x2-inch layers (taller four-tier stack) or two 8-inch layers (wider two-tier)?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Source allows either pan set; layer count and diameter change reference geometry, stack height, and cross-section scale locks for Seedance.

clarifyingQuestions.question: For final decoration, do you want only smooth sides with a piped rosette border, or additional elements (fresh rose petals, macarons, naked-style partial sides)?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Source says decorate as desired; garnish choices affect FinalDishVisual and whether segment-07 can stay motionless without drift.
